ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for instant sweetened iced coffee sachets. FOODON_03301236 (coffee beverage) is the closest parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL (Coffee and coffee substitutes) is the closest parent; no specific code exists for instant sweetened iced coffee sachets.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 33635003 (Coffee substance) is the closest available anchor; no specific concept exists for instant sweetened iced coffee.

serving.description: 1 stick sachet prepared as directed (approximately 240 ml / 240 g as consumed)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is one VIA Iced Coffee stick sachet dissolved in approximately 240 ml of cold water, yielding a lightly sweetened ready-to-drink iced coffee beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks VIA Instant Iced Coffee — Nutrition Facts (USDA Branded Food)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Starbucks VIA Instant Iced Coffee. Values are reported per 100 g of prepared beverage (one sachet dissolved in 240 ml water). Per-serving label values (energy 45 kcal, protein 0 g, fat 0 g, carbohydrate 11 g, sugar 11 g, sodium 60 mg per 240 ml serving) were converted to per-100 g basis: energy ~18.75 kcal, carbohydrate ~4.58 g, sugar ~4.58 g, sodium ~25 mg. Dietary fibre is 0 g per label. Saturated fat 0 g per label.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of instant coffee with sugar — analogous estimate based on lightly sweetened instant coffee beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks VIA Iced Coffee. GI estimated at 29 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for lightly sweetened instant coffee beverages. Coffee itself has a negligible GI; the small amount of added sugar (approximately 11 g per 240 ml serving) contributes a modest glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (29 × 11 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 3.2, rounded to 3.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~60 mg per serving); caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: With only approximately 60 mg sodium per serving, VIA Iced Coffee poses minimal sodium-related hypertension risk, but individuals sensitive to caffeine should monitor blood pressure response as caffeine can transiently raise systolic pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar (approximately 11 g per serving) contributing to glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each serving contains approximately 11 g of added sugar with a glycemic load of approximately 3, which is low; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for this sugar within their daily carbohydrate budget and consider unsweetened coffee alternatives to minimise postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat and zero cholesterol; moderate caffeine intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: VIA Iced Coffee contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, making it neutral from a lipid perspective; moderate habitual coffee consumption has been associated with reduced cardiovascular risk in epidemiological studies, though the added sugar should be considered in the context of overall dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Starbucks VIA Iced Coffee is a commercially produced instant iced coffee product sold in individual stick sachets, typically in a 5-stick pack. Each sachet is dissolved in cold water to produce a lightly sweetened, smooth, and refreshing ready-to-drink iced coffee beverage without the need for brewing equipment or refrigeration prior to preparation. The product is an ultra-processed beverage combining instant coffee with a small amount of sugar and natural flavours. Per 240 ml serving, it delivers approximately 45 kcal, 11 g of carbohydrate (all from added sugar), 0 g protein, 0 g fat, and 60 mg sodium. The beverage is dairy-free and suitable for vegans. In Singapore, it is available through Starbucks outlets and food delivery platforms such as field survey. Clinically, the low glycemic load (approximately 3 per serving) makes it relatively benign for blood glucose management compared to sweetened milk-based coffee drinks, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should still account for the added sugar. The caffeine content may transiently elevate blood pressure in sensitive individuals. The product contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, making it cardiovascularly neutral from a lipid standpoint.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from the USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods database entry for Starbucks VIA Instant Iced Coffee, which is an approved secondary global source. The match is treated as exact for the branded product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Protein_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods label data; this instant coffee beverage contains no protein by product formulation. Fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods label data; naturally zero as this is a sugar-and-coffee beverage with no fat-containing ingredients. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all 0.0: confirmed zero by product formulation. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per label; coffee beverages of this type contain no dietary fibre. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero by product formulation. Potassium_mg is -1.0: not reported on the product nutrition label and not available in the USDA Branded Foods entry reviewed. Calcium_mg is -1.0: not reported on the product nutrition label. Iron_mg is -1.0: not reported on the product nutrition label. Vitamin_c_mg is -1.0: not reported in source. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1.0: not reported in source. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1.0: not reported in source. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1.0: not reported in source. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1.0: not reported in source. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1.0: not reported in source. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1.0: not reported in source. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1.0: not reported in source. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1.0: not reported in source. Vitamin_e_mg is -1.0: not reported in source. Phosphorus_mg is -1.0: not reported in source. Magnesium_mg is -1.0: not reported in source. Zinc_mg is -1.0: not reported in source. Selenium_mcg is -1.0: not reported in source. Water_g is estimated at 94.0 g per 100 g of prepared beverage based on the low total dissolved solids content. GI is an analogous estimate of 29 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for lightly sweetened instant coffee; no direct GI measurement exists for this branded product. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this product as it is a US-origin branded commercial beverage sold globally; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese local-language names are conventionally used. The field survey listing price of SGD 8.70 for a 5-stick pack is preserved from the input record. Halal and kosher certification status is not confirmed from available sources and has been set to false pending verification.
